data.title: WebCatalog: Turn Websites into Desktop Apps
data.excerpt: Discover how WebCatalog boosts productivity by turning any website into a desktop app. Manage multiple accounts effortlessly—try it today!

$media.alt: WebCatalog: Turn Websites into Desktop Apps

children.text: If you're tired of cluttered browser tabs and the hassle of switching between multiple accounts, WebCatalog is here to revolutionize your workflow. This tool turns any website into a native desktop app, allowing you to manage multiple accounts, organize workspaces, and access web apps from a single, streamlined interface. I've been using it for months, and it's a game-changer for productivity.
Try WebCatalog here
and see the difference for yourself.
*Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links, which means I may earn a commission if you make a purchase through them, at no extra cost to you.*
What is WebCatalog and Why Does It Matter?

WebCatalog is a powerful platform that transforms how you interact with web-based tools. Instead of juggling dozens of browser tabs, you can convert sites like Gmail, Slack, or Trello into standalone desktop applications. This not only reduces clutter but also enhances focus by providing dedicated windows for each app. What sets WebCatalog apart is its ability to handle multiple accounts simultaneously—perfect for freelancers or teams managing different client profiles. Learn more about
what WebCatalog is
and how it simplifies digital workflows.
Key Features That Make WebCatalog Stand Out

WebCatalog isn't just about turning websites into apps; it's packed with features that elevate productivity. First, its multi-account management lets you log into several instances of the same service without conflicts. Second, workspace organization allows you to group apps by project or workflow, so you can switch contexts with a click. Third, it offers a curated catalog of desktop apps, making discovery easy. The platform is lightweight and fast, ensuring apps launch quickly without draining resources.
Explore why WebCatalog shines
compared to other tools. With regular updates, it's always improving—for instance, recent enhancements include better synchronization and customizable shortcuts.
Discover WebCatalog's latest features
to supercharge your setup.
New and Highlighted Features to Get Excited About

WebCatalog consistently rolls out updates that make it even more indispensable. One standout is the improved app isolation, which ensures each desktop app runs in its own sandbox, preventing data leaks and enhancing security. Another highlight is the enhanced workspace templates, where you can save and share setups with your team—ideal for onboarding. The latest version also includes deeper integration with popular services like ClickUp and monday.com, streamlining project management. For a hands-on guide, check out
how to use WebCatalog with ClickUp
or
monday.com
. These features aren't just incremental; they're designed to save you hours each week by reducing context switching and minimizing distractions.
Who is WebCatalog Ideal For?

WebCatalog is perfect for anyone who relies on multiple web apps daily. Freelancers and remote workers will love how it keeps different client accounts separate and organized. Marketing teams can manage social media tools without overlap, while developers can run various SaaS platforms side-by-side. It's also great for students juggling research tools and project management apps. If you use services like Apollo.io for sales, WebCatalog can help you maintain multiple prospecting accounts seamlessly—learn more with
using Apollo.io in WebCatalog
. Essentially, if you value efficiency and hate tab overload, WebCatalog is your go-to solution. See real-world
use cases for WebCatalog
to inspire your setup.
How to Get Started with WebCatalog

Getting started is straightforward. Download WebCatalog from the official site, install it, and then use the app catalog to add websites as desktop apps. You can customize each app with icons, notifications, and workspace assignments. Pro tip: use keyboard shortcuts to switch between apps lightning-fast. For a step-by-step walkthrough, refer to our
how-to-use-webcatalog guide
. The process takes minutes, and you'll notice an immediate boost in focus and productivity. Don't just take my word for it—
sign up for WebCatalog today
and transform your digital workspace. Many users report saving over an hour daily by eliminating tab clutter and streamlining account access.

FAQs
Q: Is WebCatalog free to use?

A: WebCatalog offers a free tier with core features, including turning websites into apps and basic multi-account support. For advanced features like workspace templates and priority support, premium plans are available at competitive prices.
Q: Can I use WebCatalog on multiple devices?
A: Yes, WebCatalog is available for Windows, macOS, and Linux, and your settings can sync across devices with a premium subscription, ensuring a consistent experience everywhere.
Q: How does WebCatalog handle security?

A: WebCatalog isolates each app in its own sandbox, preventing cross-app data leaks and enhancing privacy. It doesn't store your login credentials, relying instead on secure browser sessions for authentication.
